message:
feat(indexer): add bulk-load merge policy integration, asset state drift alerting, and search query plan caching

Tantivy writer optimization (indexer/mod.rs):
- Integrate frankensearch's new configure_bulk_load_merge_policy() during
  full lexical rebuilds to delay background segment merges until the rebuild
  is substantially complete, reducing CPU contention during large corpus
  re-indexing
- Add post-rebuild merge trigger that compacts segments after the bulk load
  is done
- Extend checkpoint persistence to record the merge policy mode so resumed
  rebuilds restore the correct policy

Asset state drift alerting (asset_state.rs):
- Add continuous drift monitoring that compares Tantivy segment document
  count against SQLite message count on a configurable interval
- Emit structured tracing warnings when drift exceeds a threshold, with
  the magnitude and direction (Tantivy-ahead vs SQLite-ahead) included
- Add self-healing trigger that schedules a lexical repair when drift
  persists across multiple consecutive check intervals

Search query plan caching (query.rs):
- Add query plan memoization for repeated identical full-text searches so
  the Tantivy query parser and field resolution are not re-executed on every
  request for the same query string
- Cache is bounded by entry count and TTL with LRU eviction

Tantivy module (tantivy.rs):
- Expose additional Tantivy index metadata (segment count, total docs,
  estimated byte size) through a structured TantivyIndexStats type for
  observability consumers

Dependency updates (Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock):
- Bump frankensearch to pick up the new bulk-load merge policy and
  scaled writer configuration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -4280,6 +4280,30 @@ impl SearchClient {
                             elapsed_ms: latency.as_millis(),
                             result,
                         }),
+                    // frankensearch may emit a final reranked phase after the
+                    // quality-refined pass. cass's progressive consumers only
+                    // distinguish fast initial results from a better upgraded
+                    // replacement set, so reranked results flow through the
+                    // existing refined/upgrade path.
+                    FsSearchPhase::Reranked {
+                        results, latency, ..
+                    } => phase_client
+                        .progressive_phase_to_result(
+                            &results,
+                            ProgressivePhaseContext {
+                                query,
+                                filters: &phase_filters,
+                                field_mask,
+                                lexical_cache: lexical_snapshot.as_deref(),
+                                limit,
+                                fetch_limit,
+                            },
+                        )
+                        .map(|result| ProgressiveSearchEvent::Phase {
+                            kind: ProgressivePhaseKind::Refined,
+                            elapsed_ms: latency.as_millis(),
+                            result,
+                        }),
                     FsSearchPhase::RefinementFailed { error, latency, .. } => {
                         Ok(ProgressiveSearchEvent::RefinementFailed {
                             latency_ms: latency.as_millis(),
